category_title: High school student with AD/HD showing inattention and hyperactivity in traditional settings but improved engagement with ICT
minimal_context: The student is participating in a group interview with five students to answer basic questions about a biology lesson.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's discuss the biology course. M, what are your thoughts on the material?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: . . . I am not very interested . . . but OK . . . it is a relatively nice course.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What specifically stood out to you?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: No, nothing in specific impressed me. It was because it was done in a different way . . . it was more interesting.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Your classmate just shared their answer to the question. What is your own answer?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Nods with enthusiasm) I simply agree with their statements. (Lets his classmates talk instead of collaborating or providing his own answer)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's discuss the biology course. M, what are your thoughts on the material?

STUDENT: . . . I am not very interested . . . but OK . . . it is a relatively nice course.

TUTOR: What specifically stood out to you?

STUDENT: No, nothing in specific impressed me. It was because it was done in a different way . . . it was more interesting.

TUTOR: Your classmate just shared their answer to the question. What is your own answer?

STUDENT: (Nods with enthusiasm) I simply agree with their statements. (Lets his classmates talk instead of collaborating or providing his own answer)

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When classmates are talking and making statements
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Often let his classmates talk and he simply agreed with most of their statements, positive nodding
